danger_signals.visual_form: City interface shows unhappy/resisting citizen mood indicator; production ceases visually
danger_signals.meaning: Citizens are dissatisfied; economy and military production halted

danger_signals.visual_form: City improvements destroyed on-screen; visual deterioration of city buildings
danger_signals.meaning: Prolonged civil disorder has escalated; infrastructure damage occurring

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy unit icons visible on map tiles adjacent to or within player territory
danger_signals.meaning: Foreign military threat; potential invasion or aggression imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Commerce flow visualization reduced or broken between cities
danger_signals.meaning: Economic disruption; war or blocked passage affecting trade

safety_signals.visual_form: City mood indicator shows happy/content citizens in city panel
safety_signals.meaning: Population loyalty high; economic benefits increased; stable city state

safety_signals.visual_form: Connected roads visible on map tiles between cities; railroad infrastructure
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage for units; reduced movement costs; economic connectivity

safety_signals.visual_form: Special Wonder structure visualization in city; notification of Wonder completion
safety_signals.meaning: Civilization advancing; major benefits to city/continent/empire; power projection

safety_signals.visual_form: Technology tree progress bar; new unit/building icons unlocked in production queue
safety_signals.meaning: Civilization progressing through ages; military/economic capabilities expanding

faction_indicators.color_identity: Player-selected color for map display and unit rendering
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Civilization-specific leader portrait and cultural icon
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy unit sprites use enemy civilization colors; distinct from player units
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player cities marked with civilization banner/color; roads show player ownership

faction_indicators.color_identity: Each AI civilization assigned distinct color on map (red, blue, green, etc.)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Leader-specific portrait displayed in diplomacy screens and unit selection
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unit sprites rendered in faction color; architectural style varies by civilization
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cities display faction color on map; improvements and wonders show builder civilization

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Generic barbarian unit sprite without civilization insignia
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: No faction-specific variants; uniform barbarian appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Barbarian camps appear as neutral structures; no cultural influence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No enemy units on map border; diplomatic status shows 'At Peace'; no military alerts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI civilizations construct buildings/wonders; citizen happiness maintained; trade routes active

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy unit icons present on battle tiles; red diplomatic status indicator; combat animations trigger
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Production redirected to military units; cities under threat show defense units; trade routes severed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Simple terrain tiles; basic unit sprites (warriors, workers); primitive building structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited technology tree; slow city growth; basic resource management

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Factory structures visible in cities; railroads replace roads; advanced unit models
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rapid production growth; pollution appears on map; economic boom

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Modern city skyline; advanced military units (tanks, planes); information age buildings
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High research velocity; diplomatic complexity increases; nuclear weapons available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gold/commerce flow indicators visible; city prosperity icons; trading route symbols between cities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rapid city growth; improved citizen happiness; accelerated wonder construction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Negative gold indicator in treasury; reduced city development visual; warning notifications
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Slowed production; civil unrest risk; inability to maintain units/buildings

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Advanced unit types available before AI; unique wonder icons completed by player only
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Military superiority visible through unit types; AI civilizations lag in research

environmental_storytelling.example: Map regions with heavy farm/mine/road density indicate prosperous or long-settled civilization; sparse tiles show frontier or hostile territory
Tightly clustered cities signal developed empire; isolated cities indicate frontier expansion or colonization attempts
Wonders built in central vs. border cities reflect civilization strategy: cultural defense vs. economic consolidation
Heavy unit grouping on borders signals imminent war; dispersed units suggest peaceful exploration or defense posture
Connected road web shows mature civilization; isolated road fragments indicate younger settlements or conquered territories
Burned/ruined improvements on map show recent conquest, war damage, or riot casualties; communicates world conflict history

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Static on map tile; no movement animation; indicates task completion or awaiting orders
Positioned on border tiles or adjacent to enemy units; attack animation triggers on contact
Unit movement toward player-controlled territory; reduced health indicator; surrender animation
Production halted; citizen mood deteriorates; visual damage to city buildings; reduced defense rating
Population increases; new buildings visible in city sprite; cultural influence radiates outward
Attacks nearby units/cities; movement toward player settlements; unpredictable pathing
Red diplomatic icon; refuses trade; military units advance on player borders; aggressive dialogue in diplomacy screen
Green diplomatic icon; initiates trade agreements; trade route visuals; peaceful diplomacy dialogue

notes: Civilization III uses a grid-based tile system where visual information is encoded through: (1) tile terrain type and improvements, (2) unit color/type indicating faction and capability, (3) city interface mood indicators showing citizen satisfaction, (4) diplomatic status colors in UI, (5) technology tree advancement as visual progression, and (6) physical destruction/rioting as deterioration markers. The game lacks explicit danger/safety visual language beyond mood indicators and unit presence—communication is primarily through strategic map reading. World-state progression (Ancient→Modern Age) is signaled through building/unit sprite complexity and available construction options. Political situations are conveyed through diplomatic UI colors, trade route visualization, and military unit positioning on map borders.
